gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/tph1/enable.snippet: Symbol 'tph-1' resolved to WB:WBGene00006600 (C. elegans tryptophan hydroxylase). Figure Panel E: labeled above the ADF neuron (blue triangle) in the NEURONS compartment.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/ser6/enable.snippet: Symbol 'ser-6' resolved to WB:WBGene00021897 (C. elegans biogenic amine GPCR). Figure Panel E: labeled above the AWB neuron (green triangle) in the NEURONS compartment.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/mod1/enable.snippet: Symbol 'mod-1' resolved to WB:WBGene00003386 (C. elegans serotonin-gated chloride channel). Figure Panel E: labeled above the URX neuron (yellow hexagon) in the NEURONS compartment.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/tbh1/enable.snippet: Symbol 'tbh-1' resolved to WB:WBGene00006541 (C. elegans tyramine beta-hydroxylase). Figure Panel E: labeled above the RIC neuron (red hexagon) in the NEURONS compartment.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/nhr76/enabl.snippet: Symbol 'nhr-76' resolved to WB:WBGene00015497 (C. elegans nuclear hormone receptor). Figure Panel E: first node inside the INTESTINE green box, with a pointed arrow to ATGL.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/atgl1/enabl.snippet: Figure label 'ATGL' resolved to C. elegans atgl-1, WB:WBGene00015484 (adipose triglyceride lipase). Figure Panel E: second node inside the INTESTINE green box, downstream of nhr-76 and upstream of beta-oxidation/Fat loss.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/tph1/molecu.snippet: tph-1 annotated to tryptophan 5-monooxygenase activity (IBA, WB). Rate-limiting enzyme of serotonin (5-HT) biosynthesis in the ADF neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/tph1/part_o.snippet: tph-1 annotated to serotonin biosynthetic process (IBA, WB). Produces the 5-HT signal released by ADF.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/ser6/molecu.snippet: ser-6 annotated to octopamine receptor activity (IDA, WB). Consistent with Panel E showing octopamine (Oct) from tbh-1/RIC acting on ser-6 in AWB.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/ser6/part_o.snippet: ser-6 annotated to octopamine signaling pathway (IDA, WB).

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/mod1/molecu.snippet: mod-1 annotated to serotonin-gated chloride channel activity (IDA, WB). Ionotropic serotonin receptor; receives the 5-HT signal in URX.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/mod1/part_o.snippet: mod-1 annotated to serotonin receptor signaling pathway (IDA/IMP, WB).

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/tbh1/molecu.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to tyramine-beta hydroxylase activity (IMP/ISS/IBA, WB). Converts tyramine to octopamine in the RIC neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/tbh1/part_o.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to octopamine biosynthetic process (IMP, WB). Produces the octopamine (Oct) signal released by RIC.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/nhr76/molec.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to nuclear receptor activity (IBA, WB); ligand-dependent DNA-binding transcription factor acting in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/nhr76/part_.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II (IBA, WB). TF endpoint of the pathway, regulating intestinal target gene atgl-1.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/nhr76/occur.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to nucleus (IEA/IBA, WB). Active in the nucleus of intestinal cells (INTESTINE compartment in Panel E).

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/atgl1/molec.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to triacylglycerol lipase activity (ISS/IBA, WB). Adipose triglyceride lipase executing intestinal lipolysis.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/atgl1/part_.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to triglyceride catabolic process (IBA, WB). Captures the Panel E leaf arrow ATGL -> beta-oxidation / Fat loss: ATGL lipolysis liberates fatty acids feeding beta-oxidation.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/atgl1/occur.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to lipid droplet (IDA/EXP, WB). Acts on intestinal lipid droplets (INTESTINE compartment in Panel E).

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/tph1/causal.snippet: tph-1 directly positively regulates mod-1: tryptophan hydroxylase in ADF produces serotonin (5-HT; CHEBI:28790), the small-molecule regulator that activates the MOD-1 serotonin-gated chloride channel in URX. Solid arrow labeled '5-HT' (ADF->URX) in Panel E. Noble, Stieglitz & Srinivasan 2013 define this integrated serotonergic circuit controlling body fat.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/tbh1/causal.snippet: tbh-1 directly positively regulates ser-6: tyramine beta-hydroxylase in RIC produces octopamine (Oct; CHEBI:17134), the small-molecule regulator that activates the SER-6 octopamine receptor in AWB. Solid arrow labeled 'Oct' (RIC->AWB) in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/ser6/causal.snippet: ser-6 is causally upstream of, positive effect, tph-1: octopaminergic SER-6 signaling in AWB promotes serotonergic tph-1 activity in ADF within the integrated 5-HT/octopamine circuit. Mechanism between the two neurons not resolved, hence the general positive-effect relation. NOTE: vision pass flagged this arrow low-confidence/unconfirmed (intent confidence 0.3) - curator should verify the AWB->ADF arrowhead in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/mod1/causal.snippet: mod-1 indirectly positively regulates tbh-1: the MOD-1 serotonin channel in URX feeds back onto octopaminergic RIC (tbh-1) within the integrated circuit. Dashed arrow (URX->RIC) in Panel E denotes an indirect/modulatory connection; sign read as positive. Intervening neurons/steps make this indirect.

Endocrine-signal edge: the URX neuron circuit releases an endocrine signal that travels to the INTESTINE. Modeled as mod-1 activity (URX, most downstream neuronal node) indirectly positively regulating the nhr-76 nuclear-receptor activity (intestine, first intestinal node). Dashed arrow labeled 'endocrine signal' (URX->INTESTINE) in Panel E; endocrine transport across cells is upstream of intestinal signal transduction, hence indirect. Noble et al. 2013 title: '...directs the release of an endocrine signal to control C. elegans body fat.'

gomodel:run-figure1-perclaim/nhr76/causa.snippet: Panel E: solid pointed arrow from nhr-76 to ATGL inside the INTESTINE green box. nhr-76 is a nuclear-receptor/DNA-binding transcription factor, so it indirectly positively regulates atgl-1 (transcription + translation are the intervening module; TF->target = indirect per GO-CAM convention). 'nhr-76 indirectly positively regulates atgl-1.' No specific PMID found linking nhr-76 transcriptional control of atgl-1 - flagged for curator verification.
